evals.prompt: Create a rackula layout file for my homelab with a 42U 4-post-cabinet rack that has a Dell R650 1U server at U42, a Ubiquiti 48-port PoE switch at U40, and two 4U storage NAS devices at U30-33 and U26-29. Also add a 2U APC UPS at the bottom (U1-2) with va_rating of 3000 and a 24-port patch panel at U3. Include proper device_types with manufacturer, model, part_number, interfaces, and power_ports for the UPS.
evals.expected_output: Valid .rackula.yaml file with all device types properly defined including component arrays
expectations: Uses modern top-level racks array, not legacy rack object
Places requested devices using modern internal 1/6U positions
Defines device types for Dell server, Ubiquiti switch, NAS, APC UPS, and patch panel
UPS device type includes va_rating 3000 and at least one power port
Device type slugs are unique and valid kebab-case

evals.prompt: I need a rack layout file for a 2-post 20U wall-mount rack with 5 generic 1U servers stacked starting at U1, a 24-port patch panel at U6, and a 1U switch at U7 with front-to-rear airflow. Make the switch half-depth (is_full_depth: false). Add a blank 2U panel at U8-9 to fill space.
evals.expected_output: Valid .rackula.yaml file with wall-mount form_factor and half-depth device
expectations: Uses modern top-level racks array with a 20U rack
Uses wall-mount form_factor for the requested wall-mount rack
Places servers U1-U5, patch panel U6, switch U7, and blank U8 using internal positions
Switch device type is half-depth and front-to-rear airflow
Blank panel is 2U and category blank

evals.prompt: Add a new blade chassis to my-layout.rackula.yaml - put a 4U blade chassis at position 20 with 4 device bays. Then add 2 blade servers inside it at Bay 1 and Bay 2 with names 'Production Blade 1' and 'Production Blade 2'. The chassis should have colour #8BE9FD and category server, the blades should have colour #BD93F9.
evals.expected_output: Modified YAML with parent/child device relationship properly configured
expectations: Preserves modern top-level racks array
Adds a 4U blade chassis device type with requested color and category
Adds blade server device type with requested color
Adds two named production blade placements
Does not create rack-level devices at position 0; zero-position children use container_id
Device bay positions, if present, are strings for schema compatibility

evals.prompt: Create a rackula file for a small office server room with a 42U open-frame rack containing: a 3U UPS at the bottom with va_rating 5000, nema-l5-30p input type and 4500W maximum_draw; two 2U servers at U4-5 and U6-7 (front-to-rear airflow, 750W power ports); a 1U switch at U8 with side-to-rear airflow and 48 gigabit interfaces plus one 10G uplink interface. Include serial numbers, asset tags, and notes for each device.
evals.expected_output: Complete rackula file with power calculations, detailed interfaces, and extension fields
expectations: Uses modern top-level racks array with 42U open-frame rack
Places UPS, servers, and switch at requested internal positions
UPS has va_rating 5000 and nema-l5-30p input with 4500 maximum_draw
Switch defines 48 gigabit interfaces plus one 10G uplink interface
Device types include serial numbers, asset tags, and notes

evals.prompt: I have a rackula file called my-layout.rackula.yaml. Change the display_mode to 'image-label' and set show_labels_on_images to true. Also add a custom_field called 'department' with value 'infrastructure' to the rack's notes or metadata.
evals.expected_output: Modified settings section with display_mode change and custom_fields added
expectations: Sets settings.display_mode to image-label
Sets settings.show_labels_on_images to true
Adds department custom field with value infrastructure to metadata or a rack
Preserves racks and device_types from the original layout
Device bay positions, if present, are strings for schema compatibility
